title: Rigor: Prop lower surjectivity witness is missing (deferred to nonexistent computation)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Rigor gap: Prop. `lower` surjectivity defers its witness to a nonexistent §5 computation.**

In the "main case" the proof reduces $\mathrm{rank}\,\widetilde S=8$ to surjectivity of the linear map $\Phi:\bigoplus_\alpha\mathbb R^3\to\mathbb R^8$ and then says "an explicit witness is given in the numerical verification (§5)." But §5 only reports aggregate rank counts; it contains no explicit witness, and the non-vanishing of the $8\times8$ minor is never established (F3).

This argument is reusable once the invariant is fixed to $\rho^{\ne}_m$ (see companion issue), so it should be completed: give an explicit choice of six indices $\alpha_1,\dots,\alpha_6$ where the $2\times2$ minors of $\binom{\ell^1}{\ell^2}$ are nonzero (exists since $\ell^1\not\parallel\ell^2$) together with an explicit specialization of the row spaces $R_\alpha$ making the $8$ generators $\ell^t_\alpha (A^{(\alpha)})^\top e$ span $\mathbb R^8$ — yielding a nonzero $8\times8$ minor polynomial.
